content:
* We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation. For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day. We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region. We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands. We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture. We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe. Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui. Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région. Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales. Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine. Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________
sentences: * We acknowledge that we are gathered on the traditional and unceded territory of the Algonquin Anishinaabe Nation.
For generations, they have been the stewards of this land, and their deep connection to it continues to this day.
We recognize the enduring presence and contributions of the Algonquin people to this region.
We also acknowledge the painful history of colonization and the displacement of Indigenous communities from their ancestral lands.
We commit ourselves to learning, understanding, and honoring the Algonquin Nation's history and culture.
We recognize the importance of working collaboratively to create a more just and equitable future for all who call this land home.
* Nous reconnaissons que nous sommes réunis sur le territoire traditionnel et non cédé de la nation algonquine anishinaabe.
Depuis des générations, ils sont les gardiens de cette terre et le lien profond qui les unit à elle perdure encore aujourd'hui.
Nous reconnaissons la présence et les contributions durables du peuple algonquin dans cette région.
Nous reconnaissons également l'histoire douloureuse de la colonisation et du déplacement des communautés autochtones de leurs terres ancestrales.
Nous nous engageons à apprendre, à comprendre et à honorer l'histoire et la culture de la nation algonquine.

Nous reconnaissons l'importance de travailler en collaboration pour créer un avenir plus juste et plus équitable pour tous ceux qui vivent sur cette terre.
________________

content:
* Making progress with bank signatory transition, should be done by the end of next week
* Rejected for a credit card
* Reached out to sponsors and compiling a tracker of companies to reach out to for 101 Week
* OSPE is out unless we offer them an online info session
* Securing $1k from Ross Video and $500 from PEO. J.L. Richards and WizePrep are in progress.
* Continuously attending PRT/TRP where we;
* Signed confidentiality form for student list
* Signed memorandum of understanding
* Discussed ways the UOSU/SÉUO can support RSGs/GÉRs by using their resources
* Confirmed with engineering shipping and receiving that we can still ship for 101 week
* Retreat accommodations booked

content:
* Meeting with VDSA (Vice Dean of Student Affairs) yesterday, talked about the processes for how to escalate issues and handling things like that. Discussed the uoZone updates (pretty much nothing they can do) and ESS/AÉG study resources (also sort of shot down but I can talk more about it if anyone wants to msg me)
* Probably going to shoot for biweekly/monthly meetings with him during the year if possible, with other meeting as necessary throughout.
* Talking points as of now are uoZone updates and faculty/ESS/AÉG study resources (probably won’t happen but I want to talk to him about it anyways)
* Setting up meeting with Jake and Dakota for a 101 week event.

content:
* Met with Eric & Aiden and several Sub-Association presidents regarding the proposed Sub-Association policy revision and integrated their feedback
* Meeting with ChESS/AÉGC President covered their compliance status and served as an in-depth onboarding
* Received 3k from EEF/FdG for uOEC/CIuO; updated my budget
uOEC Commissioner:
* Met with other chairs on committee
* Set up time for biweekly meetings
* Sent rejections to students not picked, encouraged them to still sign up
* Thinking of soft launching the event through word of mouth
* Start chit chatting guys!!
* Budget line items tbd
VP Services:
* VP Social:
* 101 Week Coordinator:
* Meeting with Engiqueers on Tuesday to discuss collab event
* Meeting with Daniel and Ashna with Jake after this to discuss external night and sub-assication games, hoping to make better event
* Booking a meeting with JMTS team captains to discuss their event, hoping to make faculty fair better by turning it more network-y and with formal presentations (need to talk with faculty)
* Ran last committee meeting and delegated tasks to all members so each person is responsible for planning at least one event
* Reached out to faculty last week regarding a room replacement for A04 but yet to hear back, they said it should be last week or this so hoping to get something soon
* Have been attending Social Round tables
VP Philanthropic:
* No big updates - waiting on committee member selection to plan potential summer events & finalize this year’s budget
* PhilRT still discussing colour run + carnival, as well as pins/patches/whatever we hand out to people who donate on shine day & waiting on shine shirt count (ty Eric & Aiden or whoever does that for me :) )
* Meeting set up w/ Jake next week for shine day
VP Equity:
* VP Francophone:
* Manager of Translations:
* N/A
VP Sustainability:
* Chair of the Board:
* BOD!
* First one happened
* Feedback on how it ran is welcome

content:
________________
Update Discussion - Mises à Jour Discussion:
* External: What’s the academic event for 101 Week? Just curious because I never heard of it last year LOL
* Jake will catch up with Nick regarding an academic specific event
* Internal: Are the timelines in the RSG/GÉR MoU accurate?
* I’m wondering if I can rely on them to know when we’ll receive levies and student lists, so that I can include dates in the Sub-Association Policy revision.
* President: Write “within one week of whenever we receive them”, so that it’s always after we receive them from the UOSU?SÉUO.
* Internal: Are we still trying to get a credit card?
* President: Yes, we’ll revisit this after we have access to the bank account.

content:
* Tshirt, table, guaranteed - $1000
* Reach out to Prep101 again
* Comms: Discord - did some research and looked at other discords, not sure if it’s worth it
* I don’t want to set this up
* Chair: Should we make the 101 Week server become the ESS server?
* Internal: I would be happy to help set up the server. Should we do it the other way around: an ESS server that is also used during 101 Week, uOEC, etc.
* Social: Wants to keep the 101 Week server separate, because it’s also used for organizing 101 Week.
* 101 Week Coord: Agrees with keeping the 101 Week server separate. What if we made the ESS server public?

content:
* Chair: 101 week guide
* Someone who is not in good standing mentioned to me that they are applying to be a guide and plan to appeal the decision to BoD/CA.
* Are we verifying that guides are in good standing?
sentences:
* Chair: 101 week guide
*
Someone who is not in good standing mentioned to me that they are applying to be a guide and plan to appeal the decision to BoD/CA.
Are we verifying that guides are in good standing?

content:
* Social: Will not be having them as a guide
* Chair: Summer office hours?
* Internal: I will only submit a promo request if there’s more interest. Currently, it’s just the Presidents and you.
* Comms: There will not be a pinned post this year, only a story highlight.
* Internal: Sub-Association Policy revision
* Sub-Association Policy 2025 Revision
* This is being discussed by BoD/CA, I’m glad it was postponed so that we have more time to discuss it.
* Does anyone have any feedback?
* I’ve spoken to a lot of Sub-Associations and heard a lot of good feedback. I’m working on integrating it.
* For the SAF/FSA, it would be in something like the NBC/BNC Business Investment Account, not anything locked like a guaranteed investment certificate. Ideally, a separate account.
* There’s disagreement with IEEE over the requirement to have a defined membership list.
* Everyone in the meeting is on the same page that our sub-associations should be composed of students from the programs that they represent.
* As a first step, requiring 75% of the execs to be engineering students makes sense.
sentences:
* Social: Will not be having them as a guide
*

Chair: Summer office hours?
*
Internal: I will only submit a promo request if there’s more interest.
Currently, it’s just the Presidents and you.
* Comms: There will not be a pinned post this year, only a story highlight.

Internal: Sub-Association Policy revision
* Sub-Association Policy 2025 Revision
*
This is being discussed by BoD/CA, I’m glad it was postponed so that we have more time to discuss it.
Does anyone have any feedback?
* I’ve spoken to a lot of Sub-Associations and heard a lot of good feedback.
I’m working on integrating it.
For the SAF/FSA, it would be in something like the NBC/BNC Business Investment Account, not anything locked like a guaranteed investment certificate.
Ideally, a separate account.
There’s disagreement with IEEE over the requirement to have a defined membership list.
* Everyone in the meeting is on the same page that our sub-associations should be composed of students from the programs that they represent.
As a first step, requiring 75% of the execs to be engineering students makes sense.

* Chair will send a message to the BoD members.
